name: Basic Divinity
recipeInstructions:
~------------------------------OPTIONALS ---------------------------------
NOTE: TO PREWARM A CANDY THERMOMETER, SIMPLY PLACE IT IN A SMALL SAUCEPAN OF COLD WATER AND BRING TO A BOIL. LET IT SIMMER UNTIL READY FOR USE.
STEP 1: Prewarm the thermometer and separate the egg whites in a large bowl equipped with an electric mixer. Return the yolks to the refrigerator, covered for another recipe. Use a glass of ice cubes and water. For dropped divinities, prepare two cookie sheets lined with greased wax paper. For squares, use a 8 x 8-inch pan lined with greased and wax paper.
STEP 2: Dissolve sugar in boiling syrup mixture (sugar, corn syrup, water, salt) in a heavy saucepan with a tight-fitting lid, stirring constantly over low heat until the mixture becomes clear, no gritty sounds occur, and the spoon glides smoothly over the pan. Increase heat to medium and bring to a boil.
STEP 3: OPTIONAL: Cover pan with wax paper and lid; steam for 2 to 3 minutes while dissolving sugar crystals. Ensure pot does not boil over by monitoring.
STEP 4: Clean any remaining crystals on sides with a hot water brush from the thermometer bath, then return the thermometer to the hot water bath to clean.
STEP 5: Beat egg whites until stiff but not dry. If using a powerful mixer, wait for large bubbles in syrup before beating; otherwise beat now when thermometer reads 240 Degrees F (115.5 C).
STEP 6: Introduce egg whites into syrup slowly and steadily while beating on low speed. Tilt the syrup pan to get the last drop without scraping; once fully incorporated, switch to a flat whip if available.
STEP 7: Continue mixing at highest mixer speed. If using less powerful mixer, mix until beaters lift candy back in ribbons that merge into batter, forming a stationary column between beaters and bowl, losing sheen/gloss, no stickiness; a spoonful dropped onto wax paper holds shape with a peak.
STEP 8: OPTIONAL: If not done after pouring, place in freezer to set or finish by hand. Once reached firm-ball stage (246-260 Degrees F /119-126.5 C), syrup will be difficult to scrape into ice water and must be forced into a ball that holds its shape under pressure.
STEP 9: Fold in flavorings and nuts using mixer or wooden spoon if thick.
STEP 10: Drop onto wax paper-covered cookie sheets or spread in buttered pan; let cool before cutting into squares. Store in an airtight container at room temperature or refrigerator. Makes about 1 pound but appears larger than it is. DO NOT DOUBLE THE RECIPE; IT CAN BE FROZEN BUT NOT FOR EXTENDED PERIODS OF TIME.
